source_jobs.title: Trade Compliance/Export Controls Manager
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

Role Summary:

We are seeking an experienced ITAR/Export Control Manager to ensure compliance with U.S. export control laws, including the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), Export Administration Regulations (EAR), and other applicable laws governing the export of defense-related products, technology, and services. The ideal candidate will develop, implement, and manage the company’s export compliance program, oversee licensing and shipping compliance activities, as well as provide training and guidance to internal teams. This role is critical to safeguarding the company’s operations and reputation in the defense industry.

Experience, Skills, and Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor's degree in International Business, Law, Engineering, or a related field (Master’s degree or Juris Doctor is a plus).

 - 5+ years of experience in ITAR and export compliance in the aerospace and defense, and high-tech sectors.

 - Experience in developing and managing export compliance programs.

 - In-depth knowledge of ITAR, EAR, and other U.S. export control laws.

 - Proven experience in preparing and managing export licenses, agreements, and documentation.

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

 - Excellent communication and training skills, with the ability to work cross-functionally with legal, engineering, and supply chain teams.

 - Familiarity with compliance software and government licensing portals (e.g., DECCS, SNAP-R, AESDirect, SAP GTS).

 - Qualified candidate must have the ability to obtain a security clearance. This requires U.S. Citizenship

Preferred Qualifications

 - Certified U.S. Export Compliance Officer (CUSECO) or other relevant certification.



Key Responsibilities

Export Compliance Program Management

 - Develop and maintain a robust export control and ITAR compliance program.

 - Create and implement policies and procedures to ensure adherence to ITAR, EAR, and other export/import regulations.

 - Ensures that all exports, technology transfers, and defense-related transactions comply with ITAR, EAR, and U.S. sanction laws.

Empowered Official

 - Acts as the legally recognized individual responsible for ITAR compliance within the company, authorized to sign and submit export license applications.

 - Serves as the primary point of contact with the U.S. Department of State’s Directorate of Defense Trade Controls (DDTC) and other regulatory agencies.

 - Reviews and approves all export license applications, Technical Assistance Agreements (TAAs), and Manufacturing License Agreements (MLAs).

 - Provides ITAR-related training and oversees internal audits to ensure company-wide compliance.

 - Reports violations, implements corrective actions, and coordinates voluntary disclosures if non-compliance occurs.

Licensing and Documentation

 - Prepare, submit, and manage export licenses and agreements (e.g., Technical Assistance Agreements, Manufacturing License Agreements).

 - Maintain records of all export transactions and license applications in compliance with legal requirements.

Classification of Items and Technology

 - Work with engineers, R&D teams, and product managers to ensure the correct classification of products, software, and technology under ITAR or EAR.

 - Assist in determining whether items fall under the U.S. Munitions List (USML) or Commerce Control List (CCL).

Internal Auditing and Risk Management

 - Conduct regular internal audits of processes, systems, and export transactions to ensure compliance.

 - Identify and mitigate risks associated with ITAR-related activities and develop corrective actions if violations occur.

Training and Awarene

source_jobs.title: Principal Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

The Role

We are seeking a Principal Systems Engineer to support the development, integration, and verification of the Airborne Tactical Augmented Reality System (ATARS) across the full product lifecycle. This role supports the overall system development of ATARS via requirements development and traceability, architecture definition, interface management, hardware/software integration, and conducting formal design milestone reviews. The Principal Systems Engineer will operate in a cross-functional environment supporting hardware, software, optics, test, and airworthiness teams to ensure technical performance, compliance, and mission readiness of complex integrated systems.

Responsibilities

 - Develop, decompose, and manage system and subsystem requirements using approved requirements management tools.

 - Establish and maintain the Requirements Verification Traceability Matrix (RVTM), ensuring bidirectional traceability from system requirements through verification artifacts.

 - Develop and manage Interface Control Documents (ICDs) for internal and external system integrations (hardware, software, avionics, and platform interfaces).

 - Lead and support design milestone reviews, including SRR, PDR, CDR, and SVR.

 - Apply Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies (SysML) to develop system architectures, functional decompositions, use cases, and interface models.

 - Conduct trade studies and technical analysis to support design decisions and performance optimization.

 - Manage allocations of weight, power, and other system resources across components.

 - Coordinate and oversee system integration and verification activities in both lab and aircraft environments.

 - Identify and manage technical risks, ensuring mitigation plans are in place.

 - Serve as a technical point of contact across internal teams and with external partners to ensure alignment on system design and performance.

Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree in Systems Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related STEM field.

 - 8 to 12 years of experience in aerospace, defense, or mission-critical system development.

 - Firm grasp of the systems engineering development lifecycle.

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with experience in trade studies and technical decision support.

 - Proficiency with requirements management tools and products, including requirements development, decomposition, tracing, validation and verification.

 - Experience authoring and reviewing Interface Control Documents (ICDs), Concepts of Operation, Trade Studies, Documentation Trees, Engineering Management Plans, Engineering Change Packages or similar systems engineering artifacts.

 - Experience supporting or leading formal design milestone reviews (SRR, PDR, CDR).

 - Effective written and verbal communication skills for technical documentation and cross-team coordination.

 - Ability to obtain and maintain a DoD Secret security clearance.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Master’s degree in Engineering, Physics, or a related technical field.

 - INCOSE ASEP or CSEP Certification.

 - Experience with AR/VR technologies and head-mounted display systems.

 - Knowledge of MIL-STD documentation standards and defense acquisition lifecycle processes.

 - Familiarity with avionics systems, aircraft integration, or similar platforms.

 - Experience applying MBSE methodologies using SysML tools such as Cameo, MagicDraw, or Rhapsody.

 - Familiarity with airworthiness certification processes and safety-critical system development.
   
   Pay Range:
   
   $160,000-$200,000

Full-Time Empl

source_jobs.title: Principal Mechanical Engineer – Structural and Vibration Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

The Role

Red 6 Technologies is seeking an experienced Principal Mechanical Engineer – Analyst to conduct detailed analysis of advanced hardware for military jet and rotary-wing aircraft. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in structural and vibration analysis, as well as experience with thermal and CFD modeling, military environmental standards, and opto-mechanical systems.

This role provides an opportunity to work on innovative technologies in augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), and advanced optical sensor systems, helping redefine how pilots and warfighters train and operate.

Key Responsibilities

 - Lead, grow, and mentor a team of Mechanical Analysis Engineers, provide technical guidance, design leadership, and foster a collaborative and innovative work environment.

 - Perform detailed structural, vibration, and modal analyses of aerospace hardware to ensure reliability and compliance with design and environmental requirements.

 - Conduct shock and dynamic analyses, including steady-state and transient vibration regimes.

 - Conduct CFD analyses to evaluate system performance in highly dynamic fluid flow events.

 - Develop finite element models and simulations using ANSYS Workbench, verifying analytical results through test data correlation.

 - Analyze and design electronic packaging and structural components for extreme operational conditions.

 - Work closely with Mechanical, Optical, Electrical, Product, and Systems Engineering teams to develop integrated, flight-qualified hardware.

 - Ensure compliance with relevant military and aerospace standards, including MIL-STD-810, MIL-STD-516, AS9100, and MMPDS specifications.

 - Support environmental test procedure development and assist in the execution and oversight of testing programs.

 - Participate in design reviews, documentation generation, and technical reporting for internal and customer stakeholders.

Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor of Science in Mechanical, Aerospace, or Civil Engineering.

 - Strong leadership skills with a proven track record of successful team leadership, team building and project leadership.

 - 10+ years of experience in stress and structural analysis for aerospace or military systems.

 - 10+ years of experience using Finite Element Analysis (FEA) and Computer-Aided Design (CAD) software.

 - Proven experience in vibration and shock analyses, including steady-state and transient regimes.

 - Experience with electronic packaging and structural integrity assessment for aerospace applications.

 - Proven experience maintaining industrial design intent; ensuring that structural and thermal solutions align with aesthetic goals, user experience requirements, and product brand identity.

 - Proficiency in CAD software (CREO or SolidWorks Preferred) and other relevant engineering tools.

 - Hands-on experience applying MIL-STD-810 and similar environmental test method standards.

 - Familiarity with material, design, process, and quality specifications such as MMPDS and AS9100.

 - Strong communication skills and ability to document and present technical findings effectively.

 - Must be able to commute or move to Louisville, Colorado. On-site work required (relocation negotiable).

 - Qualified candidate must have the ability to obtain a security clearance. This requires U.S. Citizenship.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Master of Science in Mechanical or Aerospace Engineering.

 - Experience in the design and analysis of military hardware for airborne or defense platforms.

 - Experience designing and analyzing for weight reduction and structural optimization a plus.

 - Proficiency in ANSYS

source_jobs.title: Technical Supply Chain Manager
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

Position Overview

Red 6 is seeking an experienced Technical Supply Chain Manager to lead supply chain execution for internally designed, complex aerospace products supporting both internal production and contract manufacturing partners.

This role sits at the intersection of engineering, manufacturing, procurement, and suppliers, ensuring that critical components, assemblies, and custom hardware are sourced, qualified, and delivered in alignment with Red 6’s strict DOD, aerospace, and industry standards.

The ideal candidate has deep experience managing supply chains for complex electromechanical systems including PCBAs, harnesses, precision mechanical components, and custom aerospace hardware. This position will play a key role in managing Red 6’s blended Approved Manufacturer List (AML) strategy, where certain custom components are sourced directly by Red 6 while most materials are procured through sub-contractors and contract manufacturers (CMs).

This is a highly cross-functional leadership role requiring strong technical understanding, supplier engagement, and clear communication with senior leadership.



Key Responsibilities

Supply Chain Strategy & Execution

 - Manage the technical supply chain for complex aerospace electromechanical products.

 - Lead sourcing strategies supporting both Red 6 internal builds and contract manufacturer production.

 - Support scaling of production through supplier capacity planning and risk mitigation.

 - Ensure continuity of supply for critical components including PCBAs, custom electronics, cables/harnesses, and precision mechanical assemblies.

 - Identify and mitigate supply chain risks related to schedule, cost, or technical constraints.



Blended AML & Supplier Management

 - Manage Red 6’s direct and blended AML strategy, where custom components are sourced directly by Red 6 while other components are procured by contract manufacturers.

 - Partner with Procurement to develop and maintain the Approved Manufacturer List (AML).

 - Evaluate and onboard suppliers capable of meeting aerospace and DOD program requirements.

 - Work closely with suppliers to ensure manufacturability, quality compliance, and schedule adherence.

 - Support supplier development initiatives to improve capability, capacity, and reliability.



Contract Manufacturer (CM) Collaboration

 - Act as the primary technical supply chain interface between Red 6 and contract manufacturing partners.

 - Support CMs with sourcing of complex assemblies and components required for production builds.

 - Coordinate supply chain readiness for NPI builds, qualification builds, and production ramp.

 - Travel to CM facilities to resolve supply chain issues and support production readiness as required.

Supplier Performance & Quality Collaboration

 - Partner with the Red 6 Quality organization to ensure suppliers meet traceability, documentation, and aerospace compliance requirements.

 - Monitor supplier performance including delivery, quality, and responsiveness metrics.

 - Support resolution of supplier-driven production issues and corrective action initiatives.

 - Ensure suppliers support requirements for serialization, lot traceability, and documentation.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Work closely with Engineering, Manufacturing, Production, Supply Chain, Procurement, and Quality teams.

 - Support design teams with supplier capability assessments during product development.

 - Provide regular updates to senior leadership on supply chain risks, mitigation strategies, and supplier performance.

 - Support planning activities including material readiness for builds and production ramp p

source_jobs.title: Senior Test Manager, Systems Integration & Test
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

Senior Test Manager, Systems Integration & Test

Job Summary

Red 6 is seeking a Senior Test Manager, Systems Integration & Test to lead test strategy, test execution, hardware validation, software verification, and team development within the SEIT organization. This individual will have the aptitude, ambition, attitude, and professional experience to lead a multidisciplinary test team supporting the development, integration, verification, validation, and fielding of Red 6’s ATARS product.

As a Senior Test Manager at Red 6, you will lead the design, implementation, and execution of test plans for computer hardware, optical systems, embedded software, system interfaces, and integrated ATARS capabilities. You will work closely with design engineers, software engineers, manufacturing engineers, systems engineers, quality, program leadership, suppliers, and vendors to ensure Red 6 products meet specified requirements.

This role is both managerial and hands-on. The ideal candidate is comfortable leading people through test campaigns while also working directly with test equipment, lab environments, test fixtures, and debugging tools. This role requires familiarity with testing from the board level through subsystem, integrated system, qualification, acceptance, and manufacturing levels.

Key Responsibilities

 - Lead, mentor, and develop a multidisciplinary test team within SEIT, including hardware test engineers, software test engineers, and technicians.

 - Own and mature Red 6’s test strategy across integration, qualification, acceptance, verification, validation, manufacturing, and field test activities.

 - Develop, execute, and maintain test plans, procedures, test cases, verification artifacts, and reports for Red 6 ATARS products and integrated systems.

 - Translate product requirements, system requirements, and customer needs into testable verification methods and objective evidence.

 - Lead hardware, software, and integrated system testing from board level through system level, including computer hardware, optical systems, embedded systems, software applications, and related equipment.

 - Develop and maintain test environments, tools, infrastructure, and test assets required to support repeatable and scalable testing.

 - Partner cross-functionally with hardware, software, systems, mechanical, electrical, optical, manufacturing, and quality teams to ensure Red 6 ATARS products are testable and ready for verification.

 - Lead anomaly identification, troubleshooting, root-cause analysis, corrective action, retest planning, and closure for complex hardware, software, and system-level issues.

 - Manage test execution, schedules, priorities, resources, supplier dependencies, and cross-program test activities to support engineering, manufacturing, program, and customer needs.

 - Track, analyze, and communicate test results, metrics, risks, and readiness to engineering, product, program, and executive leadership.

 - Support technical reviews, field activities, suppliers, vendors, and test labs as needed to execute Red 6 test campaigns, including up to 10% travel.

Experience, Skills, and Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree or higher in a STEM field such as Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Systems Engineering, Software Engineering, Physics, or a related discipline.

 - 12+ years of experience in test engineering, systems integration and test, verification and validation, embedded systems test, or a closely related engineering discipline.

 - 5+ years of experience leading people, including mentoring, providing technical direction,

source_jobs.title: Contract Proposal Manager- Department of Defense
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

About the Role

Red Six Aerospace is seeking an experienced Contract Proposal Manager to lead and manage the full lifecycle of government proposals for Department of Defense opportunities. This is a high-intensity contract position structured in focused sprint cycles, typically 5–6 weeks of deep engagement. It's ideal for a seasoned DoD proposal professional who thrives under deadline pressure and wants flexibility between engagements.

What You'll Do

 - Own the end-to-end proposal process from DRFP through final RFP submission

 - Lead proposal kick-off, develop compliance matrices, outlines, and schedules, and drive the team to on-time delivery

 - Write, edit, and manage proposal volumes including technical, management, past performance, and cost narratives

 - Coordinate cross-functionally with engineering, finance, contracts, and leadership to gather inputs and ensure proposal quality

 - Serve as the single point of accountability for proposal quality, compliance, and submission

What You Bring

 - 15+ years of hands-on DoD proposal management experience, including prime and/or subcontractor roles

 - Deep familiarity with the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR), DFARS, and DoD procurement processes

 - Demonstrated experience managing proposals for Air Force, Army, Navy, SOCOM, or other DoD agencies

 - Strong working knowledge of RFP/RFQ/BAA/SBIR/STTR structures and response requirements

 - Excellent written communication skills with the ability to translate complex technical concepts into compelling proposal narratives

 - Experience with proposal tools and platforms (e.g., GovWin, GOVSHP, Privia, or similar)

 - APMP certification a plus

Contract Details

 - Structure: Sprint-based contract. Intensive 5–6 week engagement cycles

 - Location: In person- Denver

 - Clearance: Active clearance preferred; ability to obtain required



Full-Time Employee Benefits

 - Healthcare, Dental, and Vision (100% coverage for Employees)

 - Paid time off, including vacation, sick, paid holidays, and parental leave

 - Short and Long-Term Disability

 - Flexible Spending Account (FSA)

 - 401(k) Retirement Plan

 - Voluntary Worksite Benefits (Accident, Critical Illness, and Hospital Indemnity)

 - May be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based on individual and company performance, subject to plan terms.
   

Note

Red 6 reserves the right to change its current benefits program at any time, in a manner that is consistent with applicable federal and state regulations.

This job description is not a contract and confers no contractual rights, privileges, or benefits on any applicant or potential applicant. Red 6 has the right to change any and all terms of this job description, including, but not limited to, job responsibilities, qualifications, and benefits. Nothing in this job description constitutes an offer or guarantee of employment.

Red Six Aerospace, Inc, is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Director of Electrical Engineering
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

The Role

The Director of Electrical Engineering will lead and expand Red 6’s electrical engineering team in Denver, overseeing the full lifecycle of electrical hardware for our next-generation augmented reality systems. This position combines deep technical oversight with strategic leadership — driving roadmap execution, establishing design standards, and scaling a world-class electrical engineering team as Red 6 continues to grow.

This is a hands-on leadership role for someone who thrives in fast-paced, mission-driven environments and has a passion for building world-class, ruggedized electronics for aerospace applications.

Key Responsibilities

Leadership & Strategy

 - Define and execute the electrical engineering strategy aligned with Red 6’s product and technology roadmaps.

 - Lead, mentor, and grow a high-performing electrical engineering team.

 - Foster a product-forward culture within the electrical engineering team — one that prioritizes integration, manufacturability, and performance.

 - Encourage engineers to engage deeply with product intent, enabling fast, iterative collaboration with mechanical and systems teams as designs mature.

 - Partner with the VP of Engineering, CTO, and cross-functional leads (Systems, Software, Mechanical, Manufacturing) to ensure cohesive product integration.

 - Drive standardization of design processes, documentation, and testing frameworks across all electrical programs.

 - Forecast and manage resources, budgets, and project priorities for the electrical function.

 - Build, mentor, and guide a team of electrical engineers, fostering a culture of innovation, accountability, and engineering excellence.

Technical Execution

 - Provide architectural and design guidance for complex mixed-signal, high-speed digital, and power systems used in aerospace AR environments.

 - Lead electrical development for tightly packaged, mechanically constrained products — balancing thermal, structural, and electrical needs in collaboration with product-focused Mechanical Engineering.

 - Champion rapid design iteration as industrial and product designs evolve, maintaining electrical integrity through continuous refinement.

 - Drive close co-design between electrical and mechanical domains to achieve optimal product integration and manufacturability.

 - Define electrical architectures and channelization strategies for power, signal, and data distribution across airborne and simulation systems.

 - Oversee schematic design, PCB layout (rigid, flex, rigid-flex), bring-up, validation, and environmental qualification (EMI/EMC, vibration, DO-160).

 - Establish and enforce compliance with relevant aerospace and military standards

 - Collaborate closely with Systems Engineering to derive electrical requirements from top-level system specifications.

 - Lead technical reviews, risk assessments (FMEA, FTA), and trade studies to ensure reliability and manufacturability.

 - Evaluate and implement new tools, workflows, and technologies that improve efficiency, quality, and design throughput.

 - Partner with systems, mechanical, and software teams to ensure seamless electrical-mechanical integration and validation of complete systems.

Cross-Functional & External Collaboration

 - Partner with Manufacturing and Supply Chain to transition designs from prototype to production.

 - Support supplier qualification, component selection, and DFM/DFT processes.

 - Represent the electrical engineering discipline in design reviews, customer briefings, and external technical discussions.

 - Interface with suppliers and external partners to evaluate COTS components, manage subcontracted desi

source_jobs.title: Principal Electrical Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

Job Summary

As a Principal Electrical Engineer at Red 6, will lead the electrical development of next-generation augmented reality systems. You will oversee all aspects of the electrical hardware development lifecycle from requirements to design, test, and integration. You will be deeply involved in the development of new electrical system architectures. This will necessitate an ability to derive electrical subsystem requirements from higher-level system requirements.

Key Responsibilities

 - Responsible for architecting, designing, developing, modifying, evaluating, and integrating electronic equipment, electronic parts, components, and integrated circuitry for high performance and real time hardware systems.

 - Oversees all aspects of the electrical hardware development lifecycle from requirements to design, test, integration and manufacturing.

 - Evaluates various design approaches and uses analytic, simulation and prototyping methods to substantiate electrical design solutions.

 - Produces design documentation and provides engineering support to system design reviews, manufacturing, systems integration, testing and verification.

 - Contributes to proposals and estimates of budget and schedule to meet program objectives.

 - Tasks and mentors other members of the team, offering expert technical guidance to the entire electrical team.

 - Establishes and evolves best practices for the Electrical Engineering group.

Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical Engineering or related technical field.

 - Typically 12+ years of experience developing complex electrical systems for aerospace, defense, or other high-reliability applications (or equivalent combination of education and experience).

 - 5+ years of experience leading electrical architecture development for complex airborne or mission-critical systems.

 - Demonstrated expertise in subsystem electrical architecture, electrical mission systems integration, and real-time embedded hardware.

 - Experience defining electrical interfaces, power architectures, and electrical system integration strategies.

 - Experience designing and integrating electrical systems compliant with military environmental and safety standards such as MIL-STD-461 (EMI/EMC) and MIL-STD-810 (Environments).

 - Experience leading technical trade studies, architecture reviews, and multidisciplinary engineering teams to resolve complex engineering challenges.

 - Familiarity with standard lab equipment – oscilloscopes, power supplies, spectrum analyzers.

 - Ability to plan and status electrical design and development efforts to meet cost and schedule constraints.

 - Excellent communication, collaboration, and technical leadership skills.

 - Ability to obtain and maintain a U.S. Department of Defense Secret security clearance. Must be a US Citizen.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Advanced degree, Master’s or PhD, in Electrical Engineering.

 - Experience with FPGAs, SoCs, firmware, and embedded software development.

 - Experience with PCBA design and simulation software, such as Altium.

 - Experience with high-speed memory such as DDR3/DDR4 and high-speed digital interfaces such as PCIe 3.0/4.0, DisplayPort, USB 3.0, and Ethernet.

 - Working knowledge of aircraft communication protocols including Ethernet, MIL-STD-1553, ARINC 429, CAN, or similar deterministic communication architectures.

 - Thorough understanding of the systems development lifecycle in accordance with ISO/IEEE recommendations and standards such as ISO/IEEE 15288.

Estimated Base Salary Range:

$200,000 to $250,000 dependent on skills and experience

Full-Time Employee Benefits

 - Healt

source_jobs.title: Chief Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

Chief Engineer

Louisville, CO / Technology / Full-Time

The Role

Red 6 is seeking a highly experienced Chief Engineer to join our dynamic team in Louisville, CO. The Chief Engineer will play a pivotal role in driving overall system architecture, primarily focusing on hardware with a complementary understanding of software. As a deeply technical and cross-functional leader, the Chief Engineer will lead critical design decisions, conduct trade studies, and translate customer requirements into actionable system and subsystem specifications. Moreover, the Chief Engineer will collaborate with government customers, guiding the development of internal technical roadmaps to meet evolving customer needs and ensure Red 6 remains at the forefront of innovation.

Qualifications

 - 15+ years of relevant experience in system engineering, hardware design, and technical leadership roles for simulation, AR, VR, or related products in the defense, aerospace or related industries

 - Proven expertise in system architecture design, with a strong emphasis on combined hardware/software systems and systems integration

 - Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional teams and drive decisions on complex technical issues and system trades

 - Ability to perform system analysis to ensure subsystem specification, when all tied together, meet product requirements

 - Excellent communication skills, with the ability to effectively articulate technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 - Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field. Advanced technical degree preferred.

Preferred / Additional Skills

 - Experience with Sensors (camera, time-of-flight), image processing

 - Experience with virtual reality or augmented reality hardware (Rift, VIVE, Gear VR, etc.)

 - Experience working with government customers and understanding of defense industry standards and protocols

 - Experience designing complex electromechanical systems, and successfully getting those systems certified for DoD use

 - Solid understanding of requirements management tools such as Jama or DOORs

 Estimated Base Salary Range:

$200,000 to $250,000 dependent on skills and experience

Full-Time Employee Benefits

 - Healthcare, Dental, and Vision (100% coverage for Employees)

 - Paid time off, including vacation, sick, paid holidays, and parental leave

 - Short and Long-Term Disability

 - Flexible Spending Account (FSA)

 - 401(k) Retirement Plan

 - Voluntary Worksite Benefits (Accident, Critical Illness, and Hospital Indemnity)

 - May be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based on individual and company performance, subject to plan terms.
   

Note

Red 6 reserves the right to change its current benefits program at any time, in a manner that is consistent with applicable federal and state regulations.

This job description is not a contract and confers no contractual rights, privileges, or benefits on any applicant or potential applicant. Red 6 has the right to change any and all terms of this job description, including, but not limited to, job responsibilities, qualifications, and benefits. Nothing in this job description constitutes an offer or guarantee of employment.

Red Six Aerospace, Inc, is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

source_jobs.title: Director of Business Development
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

Job Summary

Red 6 is hiring a Director of Business Development to lead its Navy portfolio. This role will be based in Orlando, FL. We are seeking a dynamic, mission-focused leader who will drive Navy customer engagement and the company’s Navy strategy and growth. The ideal candidate is a former senior Navy officer with business development, pilot training, and modeling & simulation experience.

Key Responsibilities

 - Develop and execute a comprehensive Navy BD strategy that aligns with corporate growth goals.

 - Shape and influence requirements early in the acquisition cycle to align with Red 6’s capabilities.

 - Manage the full BD lifecycle: opportunity identification, capture strategy, proposal support, and contract transition.

 - Establish and maintain trusted relationships with Navy decision-makers, industry partners, and integrators.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with product, engineering, marketing, and leadership teams to ensure customer success.

 - Deliver weekly staff updates and quarterly progress reports.

 - Contribute to the evolution of BD processes, including CRM, formal capture, and proposal workflows.

Required Skills and Experience:

 - 5+ years in a business development leadership role

 - 20+ years in the U.S. Navy, preferably retired Commander rank or higher

 - Background in modeling & simulation, flight training, or LVC systems strongly preferred

 - Demonstrated success in capturing large-scale Navy contracts, preferably in emerging tech or training domains.

 - Strong knowledge of Navy acquisition programs, POM processes, and contracting vehicles (e.g., SBIR, OTA, IDIQ).

 - Exceptional communication and presentation skills.

 - Strategic thinking and execution in military BD environments

 - Customer mapping and stakeholder engagement

 - Business case development and opportunity shaping

 - Familiarity with Shipley capture methodology

 - Proposal management in both SBIR and traditional FAR-based processes

 - Relationship building with DoD acquisition offices and primes

 - General technical fluency with AR, XR, and LVC systems (no expert-level required, but must understand and communicate the technology clearly)

 - Qualified candidates must have the ability to obtain a security clearance. This requires U.S. Citizenship.



Full-Time Employee Benefits

 - Healthcare, Dental, and Vision (100% coverage for Employees)

 - Paid time off, including vacation, sick, paid holidays, and parental leave

 - Short and Long-Term Disability

 - Flexible Spending Account (FSA)

 - 401(k) Retirement Plan

 - Voluntary Worksite Benefits (Accident, Critical Illness, and Hospital Indemnity)

 - May be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based on individual and company performance, subject to plan terms.
   

Note

Red 6 reserves the right to change its current benefits program at any time, in a manner that is consistent with applicable federal and state regulations.

This job description is not a contract and confers no contractual rights, privileges, or benefits on any applicant or potential applicant. Red 6 has the right to change any and all terms of this job description, including, but not limited to, job responsibilities, qualifications, and benefits. Nothing in this job description constitutes an offer or guarantee of employment.

source_jobs.title: Contracts Manager
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

The Role

The Red 6 Contracts Manager is responsible for leading the full contract lifecycle for federal and commercial contracts. This role manages proposal support, contract review, negotiation, administration, compliance, risk mitigation, subcontract flow-downs and closeout activities. The position serves as a subject matter expert on government and commercial contracting requirements, and partners closely with business development, operations, finance, legal, supply chain, and program teams to ensure contractual obligations are understood, documented, and executed effectively.

Responsibilities

 - Manage the full lifecycle of government and commercial contracts from proposal review through award, administration, modification/renewal and closeout.

 - Review, draft, negotiate and administer agreements including prime contracts, subcontracts, nondisclosure agreements, teaming agreements, consulting agreements, licensing agreements and vendor agreements.

 - Assist in identification, development and implementation of new contract policies and processes

 - Review and analyze Requests for Proposals for contractual, administrative, and compliance requirements.

 - Evaluating and advising on project risks (e.g., costing terms, intellectual property, scope requirements, general project risks, risks posed by specific contract terms)

 - Meeting and conferring with senior leadership, program managers, and other staff on various business matters to shape strategy and data rights, discuss contractual issues, provide early identification of risks and potential liabilities, as well as recommend alternative courses of action when appropriate

 - Review proposed terms and conditions and prepare exceptions, assumptions, clarifications, negotiation positions and recommended mitigations strategies for agreements with private industry clients, government agencies, prime consultants and project partners

 - Ensure compliance with government regulations, legal requirements, and company policies

 - Maintain an audit file for each contract to include the original contract, all correspondence, changes/deviations/modifications, amendments, clarifications, payment schedules, and deliverables

 - Provide Program Managers and Executives with contract briefs during the life of contracts

 - Provide contract summaries and ensure contract execution in accordance with government and company policies

 - Provide communications, awareness, and training to Company personnel on contracts matters

 - Set up procedures and instructions specific to the Company as part of its Internal Control Program, and ensure that they are followed

 - Working knowledge of ITAR, EAR and other U.S. Regulations and international aviation-related contract management experience is a plus

 - Preferred Qualifications a plus: NCMA Certifications, including CPCM, CFCM, and CCCM

Other Responsibilities

 - You will oversee and evaluate major proposals, monitor compliance and performance on existing contracts, lead or assist in key negotiations, resolve performance issues, identify risks and associated mitigations with project managers, ensure compliance with policies and procedures as well as government and contract requirements

 - You will serve as a counterpart to Government Contracting Officer and other procurement professionals for all contract and related matters. High-trust professional relationships with Government contracting and procurement offices are critical to Red 6’s continued success, and you will be called upon to develop and foster these relationships throughout your tenure

 - Analyze material estimates, other direct costs, production costs,

source_jobs.title: Manufacturing Test Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Company Overview

Red 6 is an innovative Augmented Reality (AR) technology startup leading the way in synthetic air combat training. Our primary technology, supporting our ATARS product, enables pilots to simulate the cognitive challenges of flying while accessing realistic, scalable training in dynamic outdoor environments.

Role: Manufacturing Test Engineer

Red 6 is seeking a senior Manufacturing Test Engineer to lead development, validation, and deployment of production test systems for complex aerospace electronic assemblies. This role ensures full PCBA, subassembly and system-level test capability at both Red 6 and contract manufacturer sites.

The ideal candidate has deep experience building robust, repeatable, aerospace-grade production test environments.

Key Responsibilities

Production Test Development

 - Work with Test Development teams at Red 6 and contract manufacturers to develop, release and maintain manufacturing tests, scripts, tools and related artifacts

 - Develop PCBA, subassembly, and system-level production test strategies.

 - Define test coverage, acceptance criteria, and failure of reporting standards.

 - Work with Red 6 software development teams to integrate provided test scripts and source code.

 - Work closely with contract manufacturers and document recommended or requested functions, modules, or scripts, identify potential gaps and assure that those are implemented when possible

 - Ensure CMs properly build/compile test software for production use.

 - Develop a plan and required tools for transparency, real time availability of test logs, results, failures for each build activity

Test Infrastructure & Equipment

 - Define specifications for jigs, fixtures, and system-level test racks.

 - Oversee CM development and duplication of test racks at Red 6 and CM sites.

 - Validate test repeatability, reliability, and GR&R performance.

 - Lead environmental, functional, and stress testing validation as required.

CM & Production Support

 - Support test implementation at CM facilities.

 - Troubleshoot test failures and yield issues.

 - Drive root cause analysis and corrective actions.

 - Establish test data collection, traceability, and reporting requirements.

Compliance & Documentation

 - Ensure test processes meet DOD, aerospace, and IPC standards.

 - Develop and maintain test procedures, validation reports, and configuration control documentation.

 - Support qualification builds, FAI, and production readiness reviews.

Required Qualifications

 - 7+ years of experience in production test engineering (aerospace/defense preferred).

 - Experience with Python, C/C++, or similar test platforms.

 - Strong background in PCBA and system-level electronic test.

 - Experience developing automated test systems and fixtures.

 - Experience supporting CMs in test implementation.

 - Familiarity with compiling/building test software in manufacturing environments.

 - Strong understanding of traceability and production data management.

 - Excellent cross-functional communication skills.

 - US Citizen (security clearance is a plus).

 - Travel is required to CM facilities.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Familiarity with MIL-STD requirements.

 - Experience in environmental and stress screening (ESS/HASS/HALT)

Estimated Base Salary Range:

$128,000 to $168,000 dependent on skills and experience



Full-Time Employee Benefits

 - Healthcare, Dental, and Vision (100% coverage for Employees)

 - Paid time off, including vacation, sick, paid holidays, and parental leave

 - Short and Long-Term Disability

 - Flexible Spending Account (FSA)

 - 401(k) Retirement Plan

 - Voluntary Worksite Benefits (Accident, Critical Illness, and Hospital Indemnity)

 - May be eligible for a discretionary annual bonus based on individual and company performance, subject to plan terms.
   

Note

Red 6 reserves the right to change its current benefits program at any time, in a manner that is consistent with applicable

notes: High-tech AR startup with strong engineering focus, mostly technical roles, and some defense-related positions.
